behavior_summary: The script implements the in-game behavior for a fire pit object: it starts/stops an ambient fire sound when actors enter/leave a fast trigger region, responds to being hit (possibly igniting, playing SFX, spawning a burn effect/process, and in some NPC cases destroying the item), and provides a simple 'look' description.
U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea (U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea) marks per-item/process info state and starts an ambient looping fire SFX for the item when an actor steps into its fast-area trigger.
leaveFastArea (U8P_FIREPIT::leaveFastArea) clears the per-item/process info and stops the ambient SFX when the actor leaves the fast-area trigger.

gotHit (U8P_FIREPIT::gotHit) handles incoming hits: ignores zero-damage, checks status bits and randomness to gate ignition, may play hit/burning sounds, toggles a 'burning' status bit, creates/positions a visual/effect helper, spawns a burn/effect process after a randomized delay, and treats NPC-backed items with an alternate path that can destroy the item based on a type-flag test.
look (U8P_FIREPIT::look) composes and delivers the string 'fire pit ' to the inspecting process via Item::bark.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea: set per-item info/process-exclusion entries for the entering actor, then call playAmbientSFX to begin an ambient loop tied to the item.
leaveFastArea: clear the info/process-exclusion entries and call stopSFX to stop the ambient loop.

gotHit: early exit on zero damage; check a status bit to avoid re-ignition; perform a urandom() check to probabilistically allow ignition/hit reaction; if allowed, optionally play immediate hit SFX, set a burning status (Item::orStatus), prepare/position an effect (FREE::1A96, Item::getX/Y, Item::getDirFromCoords, METHOD::0080), spawn a burn process (FREE::1C19) with a small random delay, then clear temporary status (Item::andStatus) after suspension; NPC items follow a different branch which tests a type flag and may call Item::destroy.
look: set item info/exclude, call Item::bark with the literal 'fire pit ' addressed to the inspecting PID, and use an implies/suspend pattern for process flow control.
open_questions: Exact semantics and meaning of the status bit(s) tested/modified on the item (which precise bit(s) represent 'burning' vs other transient states) — inferred from orStatus/andStatus usage but not explicit.
Precise behavior and contents of the spawned process FREE::1C19 (what visual/functional effect it runs and its lifetime) — inferred to be a burn/particle effect but not explicit.
Meaning of the specific type-flag tested in NPC branch (which flag index and what it semantically denotes) and the conditions that cause Item::destroy to be called — inferred from getTypeFlag use.
Roles of FREE::1A96 and METHOD::0080: they appear to create and orient effect objects, but their exact operations and returned data structures are not fully known.

main_function.name: U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: Handle an actor entering the firepit's fast trigger region by marking per-item/process state and starting an ambient fire sound attached to the item.
entry_conditions: Triggered when an actor (usually the Avatar) enters the firepit's 'fast area' trigger (U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea event).
Implicit parameter: item instance (BP+06h) and the triggering actor/process id (pid) are provided by the engine when the event fires.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Event handler for fast-area entry: set per-item info/process-exclusion and start ambient fire SFX.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Event handler for fast-area exit: clear per-item info/process-exclusion and stop ambient SFX.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::gotHit
function_map.role: Event handler for being struck: manage hit/ignition logic, SFX, status bits, and spawn burn effects; includes NPC-specific behavior.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'look' action: produce the item's display text ('fire pit ').

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::playAmbientSFX
function_map.role: Engine/audio extern: start or attach a looping ambient sound effect to an object.

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::stopSFX
function_map.role: Engine/audio extern: stop a previously started sound effect on an object.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Item/display extern: make the item produce short descriptive text to a target process/player.

function_map.name: Item::getStatus
function_map.role: Item state query: read status bits/flags used to avoid redundant ignition or detect current item state.

function_map.name: urandom
function_map.role: Utility: provide a uniform random integer for probabilistic behavior gating.

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Item type query: determine if the item represents an NPC to select NPC-specific hit behavior.

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Project helper: likely allocate/initialize a temporary effect/object used when the firepit is hit (inferred).

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Project helper/spawn: spawn a burn/effect process with a delay (inferred to drive ongoing burn visuals/behavior).

function_map.name: Item::orStatus
function_map.role: Item state mutation: set status bits (used to mark the item as burning).

function_map.name: Item::andStatus
function_map.role: Item state mutation: clear status bits (used to remove temporary burning mark).

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Item info query: return item type used to select SFX variants or other conditional behavior.

function_map.name: Item::getTypeFlag
function_map.role: Item metadata query: test a specific type/flag bit used in NPC branch decisions.

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Item lifecycle: remove/destroy the item from the game world (used in NPC destruction branch).

function_map.name: Item::getX
function_map.role: Item position query: obtain X coordinate for effect placement.

function_map.name: Item::getY
function_map.role: Item position query: obtain Y coordinate for effect placement.

function_map.name: Item::getDirFromCoords
function_map.role: Utility: compute direction/heading from coordinates to orient spawned effects or facing.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Project method: apply orientation/motion/initialization to a created helper/effect (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_FIREPIT::playSFX
function_map.role: Audio: play a one-shot sound effect on the item (used for hit/ignition sounds).

internal_calls.purpose: Top-level event handler that does not invoke other internal script functions; it sets info and calls external audio APIs.
Event handler that performs cleanup and stops SFX; does not call other internal script routines.
Self-contained hit handler that uses external services and spawn but does not call other U8P_FIREPIT script functions directly.
Simple display handler that calls Item::bark externally; no internal script-to-script calls observed.

external_calls.purpose: Start a looping ambient fire sound attached to the item when an actor enters the fast area.
Stop the ambient fire sound started on entry when the actor leaves the fast area.
Display the descriptive string 'fire pit ' to the inspecting process/player.
Read item status bits to determine if ignition/hit reaction should proceed.
Perform a randomized check to probabilistically gate ignition or special hit behavior.
Play immediate hit or ignition sound effects depending on branch and item type.
Decide whether the hit target is an NPC-backed item and branch into NPC-specific handling.
Create/initialize a helper/effect object prior to orienting/positioning a spawned effect (inferred).
Fetch Y coordinate for effect placement and direction computation.
Fetch X coordinate for effect placement and direction computation.
Compute direction from coordinates to orient spawned effect/helper.
Apply the computed direction and auxiliary data to the created helper/effect (inferred initialization/orientation).
Set the 'burning' (or similar) status bit on the item before spawning the burn process.
Spawn a burn/effect process after a randomized delay to produce ongoing burn visuals/behavior (inferred).
Clear the temporary status bit set earlier after suspension/processing completes.
In the NPC branch, test a specific type flag to decide whether to destroy the NPC item.
Remove the NPC-backed item from the world when the type-flag test indicates it should be destroyed.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: The script implements the in-game behavior for a fire pit object: it starts/stops an ambient fire sound when actors enter/leave a fast trigger region, responds to being hit (possibly igniting, playing SFX, spawning a burn effect/process, and in some NPC cases destroying the item), and provides a simple 'look' description.
